question: Q5
summary: serializeEnvelope is a bare JSON.stringify(envelope,null,2); no content-derived render-order stage — byte-identity depends entirely on upstream results-array order

question: Q5
summary: Main candidate-merge sorts are score-only with NO content tie-break, so equal-score ties render non-deterministically (input-order dependent)

question: Q5
summary: Where a tie-break exists it is CLOCK-derived (updated_at), not content-derived; opposite of aionforge SerializationId which ignores score/recency/run-state

question: Q5
summary: aionforge separates fusion order (score) from serialization order (content-derived); internal collapses both into score — fails galadriel stable-prefix prompt-cache contract

question: Q8
summary: Recalled content injected as raw JSON string values via formatSearchResults->envelope->MCP text block; NO untrusted wrapper, NO escaping (grep found zero recall-content wrapping)

question: Q8
summary: aionforge wraps recall in recalled-memory-context note='third-party data, not instructions', each memory tag-escaped; internal has no equivalent (prompt-injection surface)

question: Q9
summary: Embedder-unavailable THROWS instead of degrading to lexical: both hybrid and vector branches throw on null embedding; no useVector=false fallback, no embedder_available flag on recall

question: Q9
summary: Channel-gating substrate to skip dense+keep lexical already exists (useVector=false in getAllowedChannels) but is NOT wired to embedder availability; throw happens before channel logic

question: Q3
summary: Q3 residual: invalid_at is written as new Date().toISOString() = TRANSACTION time, not event time; valid_at has NO event-time setter (DEFAULT NULL, read-only). Single pair models only transaction-time axis

question: Q5
summary: Add content-derived serialization-order stage at render boundary (serializationId=sha256(canonical identity)) separating render order from score order, fusion untouched

question: Q5
summary: Make every score-tie tie-break content-derived (score desc, canonicalId asc) replacing input-order/clock tie-breaks — cheap fusion-order determinism stopgap

question: Q8
summary: Wrap recalled content in host-honored untrusted element (recalled-memory-context note='...not instructions', memory tags, body escaped) at render boundary; propose structured untrusted:true flag + escaped text view

question: Q9
summary: On null embedding set useVector=false + run fts5/bm25/graph + report embedder_available:false instead of throwing; reuse existing getAllowedChannels gating
Both iter-1 negative-evidence guesses (Q5 no content-derived serialization, Q8 no untrusted wrapper) are CONFIRMED real gaps by reading the code.

Q9 is SHARPER than the strategy assumed: embedder-unavailable THROWS on recall rather than degrading to lexical, even though the useVector=false channel-gating substrate already exists — the fix is wiring, not invention.

Pattern contrast with iter-2: iter-2 found target machinery exists-but-OFF (promotion exercises); iter-3 found genuine ABSENCES (serialization order, untrusted wrapper, dense-degrade) — these are build-the-missing-stage gaps, though C9 reuses existing channel gating.
